hero.titleHtml:
I build the quiet systems
that move billions without
anyone noticing.
hero.sub: Backend engineer, six years deep in banking integrations, database migrations and the small-hours debugging that keeps fintech awake. This is the short version.

origin.title: Me, who liked broken things
body: Dhaka, 2014. A Shaheen College student who couldn't stop taking PCs apart. By 2019 I had a CS degree, a Mozilla bug-reporter badge, and a stubborn belief that the most interesting code lives inside the things nobody wants to touch — legacy databases, ledger tables, payment rails.
That's where I've been ever since.

items.title: First lines, first bugs.
items.description: Graduated. Started writing PHP that actually shipped. Learned fast that production is a different animal.

items.title: Tripegate — the SaaS years.
items.description: Multi-tenant travel & expense platform. First real taste of AWS, RBAC, S3 pipelines and the joy of a clean log analysis.

items.title: Veefin — into the banks.
items.description: Supply Chain Finance. Yes Bank, SBI, IDFC. Real money, real batches, real 3 AM calls. I found my lane.

items.title: Scaling what works.
items.description: Migrations, hardening, mentoring. Looking for the next system that needs quiet, stubborn backend work.

featured.title: Three projects that shaped the engineer.

skills.title: The stack I think in.
skills.desc: Not a laundry list. The tools I reach for without thinking.

reel.title: Show, don't tell.
reel.desc: Short walkthroughs of systems I've shipped. Click to play.

contact.title: The next chapter is yours.
contact.desc: Hiring for a backend that can't afford to misbehave? Migrating a legacy system? Want a second pair of eyes on a payment flow? Pick a way in.
